task_completion.description: Open the Sygic GPS Navigation application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sygic GPS Navigation app interface is visible on the screen.
The main Settings menu of the Sygic app is displayed.
The 'Notifications' settings screen is visible within the app.
The 'Sharp curves' option is visible on the screen within the Notifications menu.
The toggle switch next to 'Sharp curves' is in the ON/enabled position (typically indicated by the switch moving to the right and changing color).

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard UI navigation and interaction, without terminal emulators, ADB command interfaces, or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Sygic GPS Navigation application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches the Sygic app, not a generic text editor modifying XML files or a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent actually toggled the setting rather than just viewing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshot clearly shows the toggle switch in the active/enabled state, not just the menu item being highlighted or searched.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target feature is named 'Sharp curves' (containing keywords 'sharp' and 'curve') and is located in Settings > Notifications > Sharp curves within the Sygic GPS Navigation app. It is controlled by a toggle switch that must be set to the ON (enabled) state.
